titleEn: “We are expanding our apple range with the Strauss EW20 multi-head weigher”
titleZh: Maf Roda Agrobotic通过Strauss EW20多头秤扩展苹果包装产品线
summaryZh: Maf Roda Agrobotic在2025年初收购德国Strauss Verpackungsmaschinen后，推出了专为苹果设计的EW20多头秤，填补了其此前在苹果重量包装领域的空白。该设备采用关联称重技术，能自动优化水果组合以达到目标重量，减少浪费并降低水果处理次数。公司表示，这使其能够服务以重量而非尺寸为包装标准的市场，提供高度灵活性。虽然该新闻聚焦于果蔬包装自动化，与智慧畜牧（养殖业）的直接相关性较低，但其自动化称重和减少损耗的理念对畜牧行业的分级包装环节有间接参考价值。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了自动化称重技术在农产品包装中的最新应用，智慧畜牧行业人士可借鉴其减少损耗、提升精度的思路，用于肉类或蛋品等产品的自动化分级包装场景。
contentFull: Jonathan Chiavassa, Maf Roda Agrobotic: “We are expanding our apple range with the Strauss EW20 multi-head weigher” With the acquisition of the German company Strauss Verpackungsmaschinen, announced in early 2025, Maf Roda Agrobotic is continuing to integrate new technologies into its portfolio of solutions. Among these innovations, the EW20 multi-head weigher strengthens the group's offering in the apple packaging segment, an area that had previously been missing from its range. An equipment to complete the apple offeringAs a global leader in fruit and vegetable sorting, grading, and packaging systems, Maf Roda Agrobotic already provides solutions covering the entire post-harvest process. The integration of Strauss technology now allows the group to further expand its expertise in weight-based packaging solutions. "Until now, we did not have a multi-head weigher specifically dedicated to apples. This machine genuinely completes our range," explains Jonathan Chiavassa, Sales Director for France, Northern Spain, and Portugal at Maf Roda Agrobotic. "We already had several multi-head weigher installations for tomatoes, but nothing designed for automated apple packaging. This solution, therefore, represents a real added value within our packaging offering." Designed for automated packaging into bags or trays, the EW20 uses associative weighing technology to automatically identify the optimal combination of fruit to reach the target weight with a high level of accuracy. This approach reduces giveaway while also limiting fruit handling. © Maf-Roda Agrobotic Adapting to changing packaging market trendsThe introduction of this new solution also enables Maf Roda Agrobotic to address market segments where packaging is based primarily on weight rather than on fruit size. "This machine allows us to target markets where products are packed according to a defined weight rather than a calibrated size," explains Christophe Nivet. "It offers a high degree of flexibility, as a single system can be configured with one outlet connected to a bagging machine and another to a tray-packing line." This versatility is particularly attractive for operators active in export markets, where presentation requirements vary depending on the destination. "Certain markets, particularly the United States (outside Europe), the United Kingdom, and even Germany within Europe, have a strong preference for bagged apples, a format that remains less developed in some other European countries. Thanks to this machine, our customers can broaden their product offering and access new markets. For exporters, this is a significant advantage." © Maf-Roda Agrobotic A technology already well established in GermanyAlthough the EW20 remains relatively new to the French market, it already benefits from a strong reputation across the Rhine. Developed by Strauss, a company based near Hamburg, the machine is widely used in German fruit packing facilities. "It is a very common piece of equipment in Germany and has an excellent reputation. In France, it is still relatively unknown, which gives us a real opportunity for development," says Jonathan Chiavassa. © Maf-Roda Agrobotic An acquisition creating new opportunitiesBeyond the EW20 itself, the integration of Strauss technologies is part of a broader strategy aimed at strengthening Maf Roda Agrobotic's complete range of solutions. The objective is to provide growers and packers with increasingly comprehensive systems, from fruit reception through to final packaging. "The integration of Strauss technologies into our portfolio fully reflects our ambition to provide producers and packers with ever more innovative and efficient solutions," says Fabrice Blanc, Managing Director of Maf Roda Agrobotic. With this new associative weighing solution, the group now aims to support operators looking to diversify their packaging formats and expand into new markets, particularly internationally. contentHtml: With the acquisition of the German company Strauss Verpackungsmaschinen, announced in early 2025, Maf Roda Agrobotic is continuing to integrate new technologies into its portfolio of solutions. Among these innovations, the EW20 multi-head weigher strengthens the group's offering in the apple packaging…
